title: Desrousseauxia nodosa
intro: Desrousseauxia nodosa, also known as 'Uvira', is a plant species native to Africa, belonging to the Asteraceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, providing relief from pain and swelling. The bioactive constituents of Desrousseauxia nodosa include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Desrousseauxia nodosa is often prepared as a decoction or infused in tea form to take advantage of its health benefits, particularly for treating fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues.
